records.chapter: Chapter on the Cessation of an orphan's Orphanhood
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.5522 - Abu Abdullah (as), said: "When a girl reaches nine years of age, her wealth is handed over to her, her decisions regarding her wealth become valid, and the full legal punishments are applied for her and upon her."
records.arabicText: 5522 - وَ قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "إِذَا بَلَغَتِ اَلْجَارِيَةُ تِسْعَ سِنِينَ دُفِعَ إِلَيْهَا مَالُهَا وَ جَازَ أَمْرُهَا فِي مَالِهَا وَ أُقِيمَتِ اَلْحُدُودُ اَلتَّامَّةُ لَهَا وَ عَلَيْهَا".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5522 - Abu Abdullah (as), said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "When a girl reaches nine years of age, her wealth is handed over to her, her decisions regarding her wealth become valid, and the full legal punishments are applied for her and upon her."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Cessation of an orphan's Orphanhood
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5523 - It was narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as), that he was asked about the saying of Allah (swt), the Exalted and Glorious:
"But if you perceive in them sound judgment, then deliver their wealth to them"
(Surah An-Nisa 4:6).
Imam (as) said: "Perceiving sound judgment means the ability to safeguard wealth."
records.arabicText:
5523 - وَ قَدْ رُوِيَ عَنِ اَلصَّادِقِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : أَنَّهُ سُئِلَ عَنْ قَوْلِ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ ۝ فَإِنْ آنَسْتُمْ مِنْهُمْ رُشْداً فَادْفَعُوا إِلَيْهِمْ أَمْوٰالَهُمْ ۝
قَالَ "إِينَاسُ اَلرُّشْدِ حِفْظُ اَلْمَالِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.5523 - It was narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as), that he was asked about the saying of Allah (swt), the Exalted and Glorious:
"But if you perceive in them sound judgment, then deliver their wealth to them"
(Surah An-Nisa 4:6).
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Perceiving sound judgment means the ability to safeguard wealth."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Cessation of an orphan's Orphanhood
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5524 - In the narration of Muhammad ibn Ahmad ibn Yahya from Muhammad ibn Al-Husayn from Abdullah ibn Al-Mughira from someone who mentioned from Abu Abdullah (as), that Imam (as) said in the interpretation of this verse:
"If you perceive in them sound judgment, then deliver their wealth to them"
(Surah An-Nisa 4:6).
Imam (as) said: "If you see that they love the family of Muhammad (sw), peace be upon them, then elevate them in status."
records.arabicText:
5524 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ يَحْيَى عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ اَلْحُسَيْنِ عَنْ عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ بْنِ اَلْمُغِيرَةِ عَمَّنْ ذَكَرَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : أَنَّهُ قَالَ فِي تَفْسِيرِ هَذِهِ اَلْآيَةِ
۝ إِذَا رَأَيْتُمُوهُمْ يُحِبُّونَ آلَ مُحَمَّدٍ عَلَيْهِمُ اَلسَّلاَمُ فَارْفَعُوهُمْ دَرَجَةً ۝.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.5524 - In the narration of Muhammad ibn Ahmad ibn Yahya from Muhammad ibn Al-Husayn from Abdullah ibn Al-Mughira from someone who mentioned from Abu Abdullah (as), that Imam (as) said in the interpretation of this verse:
"If you perceive in them sound judgment, then deliver their wealth to them"
(Surah An-Nisa 4:6).
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If you see that they love the family of Muhammad (sw), peace be upon them, then elevate them in status."

records.chapter: Chapter on Those Who Are Not Prevented From Inheriting
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5620 - Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"Indeed, a newborn or an infant does not prevent you (from inheritance) nor inherits from you unless they have cried aloud. Anything concealed in the womb, even if it moves, has no share in inheritance except what the passage of night and day might change."
records.arabicText: 5620 - رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ سِنَانٍ عَنِ اَلْعَلاَءِ بْنِ فُضَيْلٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : "إِنَّ اَلْوَلِيدَ وَ اَلطِّفْلَ لاَ يَحْجُبُكَ وَ لاَ يَرِثُكَ إِلاَّ مَنْ آذَنَ بِالصُّرَاخِ وَ لاَ شَيْءٌ أَكَنَّهُ اَلْبَطْنُ وَ إِنْ تَحَرَّكَ إِلاَّ مَا اِخْتَلَفَ عَلَيْهِ اَللَّيْلُ وَ اَلنَّهَارُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5620 - Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Indeed, a newborn or an infant does not prevent you (from inheritance) nor inherits from you unless they have cried aloud. Anything concealed in the womb, even if it moves, has no share in inheritance except what the passage of night and day might change."

records.chapter: Chapter on What Has Been Reported Regarding One Who Refuses to Take His Wealth After Reaching Maturity
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5525 - Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Isa narrated from Sa'd ibn Isma'il from his father, who said: I asked Imam Ali ibn Musa Ar-Ridha (as) about the guardian of orphans whose wards have reached maturity. He offers them their rightful wealth, but they refuse to take it. What should he do?
Imam (as) said: "He must return their wealth to them and compel them to take it."
records.arabicText: 5525 - رَوَى أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عِيسَى عَنْ سَعْدِ بْنِ إِسْمَاعِيلَ عَنْ أَبِيهِ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُ اَلرِّضَا عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ وَصِيِّ أَيْتَامٍ يُدْرِكُ أَيْتَامُهُ فَيَعْرِضُ عَلَيْهِمْ أَنْ يَأْخُذُوا اَلَّذِي لَهُمْ فَيَأْبَوْنَ عَلَيْهِ كَيْفَ يَصْنَعُ قَالَ "يَرُدُّ عَلَيْهِمْ وَ يُكْرِهُهُمْ عَلَيْهِ".

records.chapter: Chapter on the Executor Withholding the heir's Wealth After Maturity, Leading Him to Commit Adultery Due to His Inability to Marry
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5526 - Muhammad ibn Ya'qub Al-Kulayni, may Allah (swt) be pleased with him, narrated from Muhammad ibn Yahya from Muhammad ibn Al-Husayn from Muhammad ibn Qays from someone who narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said regarding a man who died and appointed someone as the guardian of his affairs while leaving behind a young son.
When the boy grew up, he went to the guardian and said: "Return my wealth to me so I can get married," but the guardian refused. The boy then went and committed adultery.
Abu Abdullah (as), said: "Two-thirds of the sin of this man's adultery is upon the guardian who withheld his wealth and did not give it to him, which would have allowed him to marry."
records.arabicText: 5526 - رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ يَعْقُوبَ اَلْكُلَيْنِيُّ رَضِيَ اَللَّهُ عَنْهُ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ يَحْيَى عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ اَلْحُسَيْنِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ قَيْسٍ عَمَّنْ رَوَاهُ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : فِي رَجُلٍ مَاتَ وَ أَوْصَى إِلَى رَجُلٍ وَ لَهُ اِبْنٌ صَغِيرٌ فَأَدْرَكَ اَلْغُلاَمُ وَ ذَهَبَ إِلَى اَلْوَصِيِّ فَقَالَ لَهُ رُدَّ عَلَيَّ مَالِي لِأَتَزَوَّجَ فَأَبَى عَلَيْهِ فَذَهَبَ حَتَّى زَنَى قَالَ "يُلْزَمُ ثُلُثَيْ إِثْمِ زِنَى هَذَا اَلرَّجُلِ ذَلِكَ اَلْوَصِيُّ اَلَّذِي مَنَعَهُ اَلْمَالَ وَ لَمْ يُعْطِهِ فَكَانَ يَتَزَوَّجُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5526 - Muhammad ibn Ya'qub Al-Kulayni, may Allah (swt) be pleased with him, narrated from Muhammad ibn Yahya from Muhammad ibn Al-Husayn from Muhammad ibn Qays from someone who narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said regarding a man who died and appointed someone as the guardian of his affairs while leaving behind a young son.
records.thaqalaynMatn:
When the boy grew up, he went to the guardian and said: "Return my wealth to me so I can get married," but the guardian refused. The boy then went and committed adultery.
Abu Abdullah (as), said: "Two-thirds of the sin of this man's adultery is upon the guardian who withheld his wealth and did not give it to him, which would have allowed him to marry."

records.chapter: Chapter on What Has Been Reported Regarding One Who Makes a Bequest or Frees a Slave While in Debt
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5527 - Muhammad ibn Abi Umayr narrated from Jamil ibn Darraj from Zakariya ibn Abi Yahya Al-Sa'di from Al-Hakam ibn Utaibah, who said:
We were sitting at the door of Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), as a group waiting for him to come out. Suddenly, a woman approached and asked: "Which of you is Abu Ja'far?"
The people replied: "What do you need from him?" She said: "I want to ask him a question."
They told her: "This is the jurist of the people of Iraq, so ask him."
She said: "My husband passed away and left behind one thousand dirhams. I had a debt upon him from my dowry of five hundred dirhams. I took my dowry and also took my share of the inheritance. Then a man came and claimed that the deceased owed him one thousand dirhams, and I testified in his favor."
Al-Hakam said: While I was pondering this, Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), came out and said: "What is this that I see you counting with your fingers, O' Hakam?"
I said: "This woman mentioned that her husband died, leaving one thousand dirhams. She had five hundred dirhams as her dowry, which she took, and she also took her share of the inheritance. Then a man came claiming that the deceased owed him one thousand dirhams, and she testified in his favor."
Al-Hakam said: By Allah (swt), I had not even finished speaking when Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), said:
"She has admitted to two-thirds of what she possesses, and she has no right to any inheritance."
Al-Hakam said: By Allah (swt), I have never seen anyone more insightful than Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as).
Ibn Abi Umayr said: The explanation of this is that there is no inheritance until debts are settled.
The deceased only left one thousand dirhams, while he owed a total debt of one thousand five hundred dirhams - five hundred dirhams to her (as her dowry) and one thousand dirhams to the man. Therefore, she is entitled to one-third of the remaining amount because of her five hundred dirham debt, and the man is entitled to two-thirds due to his claim of one thousand dirhams.
records.arabicText: 5527 - رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ جَمِيلِ بْنِ دَرَّاجٍ عَنْ زَكَرِيَّا بْنِ أَبِي يَحْيَى اَلسَّعْدِيِّ عَنِ اَلْحَكَمِ بْنِ عُتَيْبَةَ قَالَ : كُنَّا عَلَى بَابِ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ع وَ نَحْنُ جَمَاعَةٌ نَنْتَظِرُ أَنْ يَخْرُجَ إِذْ جَاءَتِ اِمْرَأَةٌ فَقَالَتْ أَيُّكُمْ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ فَقَالَ لَهَا اَلْقَوْمُ مَا تُرِيدِينَ مِنْهُ قَالَتْ أَسْأَلُهُ عَنْ مَسْأَلَةٍ فَقَالُوا لَهَا هَذَا فَقِيهُ أَهْلِ اَلْعِرَاقِ فَاسْأَلِيهِ فَقَالَتْ إِنَّ زَوْجِي مَاتَ وَ تَرَكَ أَلْفَ دِرْهَمٍ وَ كَانَ لِي عَلَيْهِ دَيْنٌ مِنْ صَدَاقِي خَمْسُمِائَةِ دِرْهَمٍ فَأَخَذْتُ صَدَاقِي وَ أَخَذْتُ مِيرَاثِي ثُمَّ جَاءَ رَجُلٌ فَادَّعَى عَلَيْهِ أَلْفَ دِرْهَمٍ فَشَهِدْتُ لَهُ قَالَ اَلْحَكَمُ فَبَيْنَا أَنَا أَحْسُبُ إِذْ خَرَجَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ فَقَالَ "مَا هَذَا اَلَّذِي أَرَاكَ تُحَرِّكُ بِهِ أَصَابِعَكَ يَا حَكَمُ " فَقُلْتُ إِنَّ هَذِهِ اَلْمَرْأَةَ ذَكَرَتْ أَنَّ زَوْجَهَا مَاتَ وَ تَرَكَ أَلْفَ دِرْهَمٍ وَ كَانَ لَهَا عَلَيْهِ مِنْ صَدَاقِهَا خَمْسُمِائَةِ دِرْهَمٍ فَأَخَذَتْ مِنْهُ صَدَاقَهَا وَ أَخَذَتْ مِنْهُ مِيرَاثَهَا ثُمَّ جَاءَ رَجُلٌ فَادَّعَى عَلَيْهِ أَلْفَ دِرْهَمٍ فَشَهِدَتْ لَهُ قَالَ اَلْحَكَمُ فَوَ اَللَّهِ مَا أَتْمَمْتُ اَلْكَلاَمَ حَتَّى قَالَ "أَقَرَّتْ بِثُلُثَيْ مَا فِي يَدَيْهَا وَ لاَ مِيرَاثَ لَهَا" قَالَ اَلْحَكَمُ فَمَا رَأَيْتُ وَ اَللَّهِ أَفْهَمَ مِنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَطُّ. قَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ وَ تَفْسِيرُ ذَلِكَ أَنَّهُ لَا مِيرَاثَ حَتَّى يُقْضَى الدَّيْنُ وَ إِنَّمَا تَرَكَ أَلْفَ دِرْهَمٍ وَ عَلَيْهِ مِنَ الدَّيْنِ أَلْفٌ وَ خَمْسُمِائَةِ دِرْهَمٍ لَهَا وَ لِلرَّجُلِ فَلَهَا ثُلُثُ الْأَلْفِ لِأَنَّ لَهَا خَمْسَمِائَةِ دِرْهَمٍ وَ لِلرَّجُلِ أَلْفَ دِرْهَمٍ فَلَهُ ثُلُثَاهَا.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5527 - Muhammad ibn Abi Umayr narrated from Jamil ibn Darraj from Zakariya ibn Abi Yahya Al-Sa'di from Al-Hakam ibn Utaibah, who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
We were sitting at the door of Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), as a group waiting for him to come out. Suddenly, a woman approached and asked: "Which of you is Abu Ja'far?"
The people replied: "What do you need from him?" She said: "I want to ask him a question."
They told her: "This is the jurist of the people of Iraq, so ask him."
She said: "My husband passed away and left behind one thousand dirhams. I had a debt upon him from my dowry of five hundred dirhams. I took my dowry and also took my share of the inheritance. Then a man came and claimed that the deceased owed him one thousand dirhams, and I testified in his favor."
Al-Hakam said: While I was pondering this, Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), came out and said: "What is this that I see you counting with your fingers, O' Hakam?"
I said: "This woman mentioned that her husband died, leaving one thousand dirhams. She had five hundred dirhams as her dowry, which she took, and she also took her share of the inheritance. Then a man came claiming that the deceased owed him one thousand dirhams, and she testified in his favor."
Al-Hakam said: By Allah (swt), I had not even finished speaking when Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), said:
"She has admitted to two-thirds of what she possesses, and she has no right to any inheritance."
Al-Hakam said: By Allah (swt), I have never seen anyone more insightful than Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as).
Ibn Abi Umayr said: The explanation of this is that there is no inheritance until debts are settled.
The deceased only left one thousand dirhams, while he owed a total debt of one thousand five hundred dirhams - five hundred dirhams to her (as her dowry) and one thousand dirhams to the man. Therefore, she is entitled to one-third of the remaining amount because of her five hundred dirham debt, and the man is entitled to two-thirds due to his claim of one thousand dirhams.

records.chapter: Chapter on What Has Been Reported Regarding One Who Makes a Bequest or Frees a Slave While in Debt
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5528 - Ibn Abi Umayr narrated from Jamil ibn Darraj from Abu Abdullah (as), regarding a man who freed his slave at the time of his death while he had a debt upon him.
Imam (as) said: "If the value of the slave is equal to the amount of debt he owes or more, then his emancipation is valid; otherwise, it is not valid."
records.arabicText: 5528 - وَ رَوَى اِبْنُ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ جَمِيلِ بْنِ دَرَّاجٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي رَجُلٍ أَعْتَقَ مَمْلُوكَهُ عِنْدَ مَوْتِهِ وَ عَلَيْهِ دَيْنٌ فَقَالَ "إِنْ كَانَ قِيمَتُهُ مِثْلَ اَلَّذِي عَلَيْهِ وَ مِثْلَهُ جَازَ عِتْقُهُ وَ إِلاَّ لَمْ يَجُزْ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5528 - Ibn Abi Umayr narrated from Jamil ibn Darraj from Abu Abdullah (as), regarding a man who freed his slave at the time of his death while he had a debt upon him.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "If the value of the slave is equal to the amount of debt he owes or more, then his emancipation is valid; otherwise, it is not valid."

records.chapter: Chapter on What Has Been Reported Regarding One Who Makes a Bequest or Frees a Slave While in Debt
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5529 - In the narration of Aban ibn Uthman, it is reported that a man asked Abu Abdullah (as), about a man who had made a will appointing someone as his executor, stating that he had a debt upon him.
Abu Abdullah (as), said: "The executor should settle the debt owed by the deceased and then distribute whatever remains among the heirs."
The man then asked: "Should the executor distribute what was bequeathed for the debt? Should the debt be collected from the heirs or from the executor?"
Imam (as) replied: "It is not to be taken from the heirs; rather, the executor is responsible for it."
records.arabicText: 5529 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ أَبَانِ بْنِ عُثْمَانَ قَالَ : سَأَلَ رَجُلٌ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ أَوْصَى إِلَى رَجُلٍ أَنَّ عَلَيْهِ دَيْناً فَقَالَ "يَقْضِي اَلرَّجُلُ مَا عَلَيْهِ مِنْ دَيْنِهِ وَ يَقْسِمُ مَا بَقِيَ بَيْنَ اَلْوَرَثَةِ" قُلْتُ فَيُفَرِّقُ اَلْوَصِيُّ مَا كَانَ أُوصِيَ بِهِ فِي اَلدَّيْنِ مِمَّنْ يُؤْخَذُ اَلدَّيْنُ أَ مِنَ اَلْوَرَثَةِ أَمْ مِنَ اَلْوَصِيِّ فَقَالَ "لاَ يُؤْخَذُ مِنَ اَلْوَرَثَةِ وَ لَكِنَّ اَلْوَصِيَّ ضَامِنٌ لَهُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5529 - In the narration of Aban ibn Uthman, it is reported that a man asked Abu Abdullah (as), about a man who had made a will appointing someone as his executor, stating that he had a debt upon him.
records.thaqalaynMatn:
Abu Abdullah (as), said: "The executor should settle the debt owed by the deceased and then distribute whatever remains among the heirs."
The man then asked: "Should the executor distribute what was bequeathed for the debt? Should the debt be collected from the heirs or from the executor?"
Imam (as) replied: "It is not to be taken from the heirs; rather, the executor is responsible for it."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Clearance of the deceased's Liability From Debt When Someone Guarantees it for the Creditors with Their Consent
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5530 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Abdullah ibn Sinan from Abu Abdullah (as), regarding a man who dies while owing a debt, and a guarantor assumes responsibility for it on behalf of the creditors.
Imam (as) said: "If the creditors agree, then the deceased is cleared of responsibility."
records.arabicText: 5530 - رَوَى اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي اَلرَّجُلِ يَمُوتُ وَ عَلَيْهِ دَيْنٌ فَيَضْمَنُهُ ضَامِنٌ لِلْغُرَمَاءِ قَالَ "إِذَا رَضِيَ اَلْغُرَمَاءُ فَقَدْ بَرِئَتْ ذِمَّةُ اَلْمَيِّتِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5530 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Abdullah ibn Sinan from Abu Abdullah (as), regarding a man who dies while owing a debt, and a guarantor assumes responsibility for it on behalf of the creditors.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "If the creditors agree, then the deceased is cleared of responsibility."

records.chapter: Chapter on a Sold Item That Remains Intact When the Buyer Dies While in Debt and Owing Its Price
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5531 - Muhammad ibn Abi Umayr narrated from Jamil ibn Darraj from one of our companions from Abu Abdullah (as), regarding a man who sold merchandise to another man. The buyer took possession of the merchandise but did not pay the price, and then the buyer died while the merchandise was still in its original form.
Imam (as) said: "If the merchandise remains in its original state, it should be returned to the seller, and the creditors have no right to dispute over it."
records.arabicText: 5531 - رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ جَمِيلِ بْنِ دَرَّاجٍ عَنْ بَعْضِ أَصْحَابِنَا عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي رَجُلٍ بَاعَ مَتَاعاً مِنْ رَجُلٍ فَقَبَضَ اَلْمُشْتَرِي اَلْمَتَاعَ وَ لَمْ يَدْفَعِ اَلثَّمَنَ ثُمَّ مَاتَ اَلْمُشْتَرِي وَ اَلْمَتَاعُ قَائِمٌ بِعَيْنِهِ فَقَالَ "إِذَا كَانَ اَلْمَتَاعُ قَائِماً بِعَيْنِهِ رُدَّ إِلَى صَاحِبِ اَلْمَتَاعِ وَ لَيْسَ لِلْغُرَمَاءِ أَنْ يُخَاصِمُوهُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5531 - Muhammad ibn Abi Umayr narrated from Jamil ibn Darraj from one of our companions from Abu Abdullah (as), regarding a man who sold merchandise to another man. The buyer took possession of the merchandise but did not pay the price, and then the buyer died while the merchandise was still in its original form.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "If the merchandise remains in its original state, it should be returned to the seller, and the creditors have no right to dispute over it."

records.chapter: Chapter on Settling Debt From Blood Money (diyyah)
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5532 - Safwan ibn Yahya Al-Azraq narrated from Abu Al-Hasan (as), regarding a man who was killed while owing a debt and left behind no wealth. His family took the blood money (diyyah) from his killer. Should they use it to settle his debt?
Imam (as) said: "Yes."
I asked: "Even though he left nothing behind?"
Imam (as) replied: "They took his blood money, so they must use it to settle his debt."
records.arabicText: 5532 - رَوَى صَفْوَانُ بْنُ يَحْيَى اَلْأَزْرَقُ عَنْ أَبِي اَلْحَسَنِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي اَلرَّجُلِ يُقْتَلُ وَ عَلَيْهِ دَيْنٌ وَ لَمْ يَتْرُكْ مَالاً فَأَخَذَ أَهْلُهُ اَلدِّيَةَ مِنْ قَاتِلِهِ عَلَيْهِمْ أَنْ يَقْضُوا دَيْنَهُ قَالَ "نَعَمْ" قُلْتُ وَ هُوَ لَمْ يَتْرُكْ شَيْئاً قَالَ "إِنَّمَا أَخَذُوا دِيَتَهُ فَعَلَيْهِمْ أَنْ يَقْضُوا دَيْنَهُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5532 - Safwan ibn Yahya Al-Azraq narrated from Abu Al-Hasan (as), regarding a man who was killed while owing a debt and left behind no wealth. His family took the blood money (diyyah) from his killer. Should they use it to settle his debt?
records.thaqalaynMatn:
Imam (as) said: "Yes."
I asked: "Even though he left nothing behind?"
Imam (as) replied: "They took his blood money, so they must use it to settle his debt."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Disapproval of Appointing a Woman as an Executor of a Bequest
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5533 - Al-Sakuni narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) from his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), from his forefathers, peace be upon them, who said that the Commander of the Faithful (as) said:
"A woman should not be appointed as an executor of a will because Allah (swt), the Almighty and Glorious, says: "And do not give the weak-minded your wealth" (Surah An-Nisa 4:5)."
records.arabicText: 5533 - رَوَى اَلسَّكُونِيُّ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنْ آبَائِهِ عَلَيْهِمُ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ قَالَ أَمِيرُ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "اَلْمَرْأَةُ لاَ يُوصَى إِلَيْهَا لِأَنَّ اَللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ يَقُولُ ۝ وَ لاٰ تُؤْتُوا اَلسُّفَهٰاءَ أَمْوٰالَكُمُ ۝.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5533 - Al-Sakuni narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) from his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), from his forefathers, peace be upon them, who said that the Commander of the Faithful (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "A woman should not be appointed as an executor of a will because Allah (swt), the Almighty and Glorious, says: "And do not give the weak-minded your wealth" (Surah An-Nisa 4:5)."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Disapproval of Appointing a Woman as an Executor of a Bequest
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5534 - In another narration, Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), was asked about the saying of Allah (swt), the Almighty and Glorious:
"And do not give the weak-minded your wealth." (Qur'an 4:5).
Imam (as) said: "Do not give it to one who drinks alcohol or to women."
Then Imam (as) added: "And who is more foolish than a person who drinks alcohol?"
records.arabicText: 5534 - وَ فِي خَبَرٍ آخَرَ : سُئِلَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ قَوْلِ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ "وَ لاٰ تُؤْتُوا اَلسُّفَهٰاءَ أَمْوٰالَكُمُ" قَالَ "لاَ تُؤْتُوهَا شَارِبَ اَلْخَمْرِ وَ لاَ اَلنِّسَاءَ" ثُمَّ قَالَ "وَ أَيُّ سَفِيهٍ أَسْفَهُ مِنْ شَارِبِ اَلْخَمْرِ".

records.chapter: Chapter on the Obligation of the Executor of the Executor in Fulfilling the Bequest
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5535 - Muhammad ibn Al-Hasan Al-Saffar, may Allah (swt) be pleased with him, wrote to Abu Muhammad Imam Hasan ibn Ali Al-Askari (as), asking:
"A man was the executor of another man's will, and then he died and appointed another man as his executor. Is the second executor obligated to fulfill the will of the man for whom the first executor was appointed?"
Imam (as) wrote in response: "He is obligated by his right if there is a rightful claim upon him, if Allah (swt) wills."
records.arabicText: 5535 - كَتَبَ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ اَلْحَسَنِ اَلصَّفَّارُ رَضِيَ اَللَّهُ عَنْهُ إِلَى أَبِي مُحَمَّدٍ اَلْحَسَنِ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : رَجُلٌ كَانَ وَصِيَّ رَجُلٍ فَمَاتَ وَ أَوْصَى إِلَى رَجُلٍ آخَرَ هَلْ يَلْزَمُ اَلْوَصِيَّ وَصِيَّةُ اَلرَّجُلِ اَلَّذِي كَانَ هَذَا وَصِيَّهُ فَكَتَبَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ "يَلْزَمُهُ بِحَقِّهِ إِنْ كَانَ لَهُ قِبَلَهُ حَقٌّ إِنْ شَاءَ اَللَّهُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.5535 - Muhammad ibn Al-Hasan Al-Saffar, may Allah (swt) be pleased with him, wrote to Abu Muhammad Imam Hasan ibn Ali Al-Askari (as), asking:
"A man was the executor of another man's will, and then he died and appointed another man as his executor. Is the second executor obligated to fulfill the will of the man for whom the first executor was appointed?"
Imam (as) wrote in response:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "He is obligated by his right if there is a rightful claim upon him, if Allah (swt) wills."

records.chapter: Chapter on a Man Who Bequeaths a Portion of His Wealth to Another and is Then Killed by Mistake
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5536 - Asim ibn Humayd narrated from Muhammad ibn Qays, who said:
I asked Imam (as) about a man who bequeathed a portion of his wealth - a third or a fourth - to another man. Then, the man (the testator) was mistakenly killed.
Imam (as) said: "The will is to be executed from his wealth and from his blood money (diyyah)."
records.arabicText: 5536 - رَوَى عَاصِمُ بْنُ حُمَيْدٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ قَيْسٍ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لَهُ رَجُلٌ أَوْصَى لِرَجُلٍ بِوَصِيَّةٍ مِنْ مَالِهِ ثُلُثٍ أَوْ رُبُعٍ فَيُقْتَلُ اَلرَّجُلُ خَطَأً يَعْنِي اَلْمُوصِيَ فَقَالَ "تُجَازُ لِهَذَا اَلْوَصِيَّةُ مِنْ مَالِهِ وَ مِنْ دِيَتِهِ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.5536 - Asim ibn Humayd narrated from Muhammad ibn Qays, who said:
I asked Imam (as) about a man who bequeathed a portion of his wealth - a third or a fourth - to another man. Then, the man (the testator) was mistakenly killed.
Imam (as) said: "
records.thaqalaynMatn: The will is to be executed from his wealth and from his blood money (diyyah)."

records.chapter: Chapter on a Man Who Bequeaths a Portion of His Wealth to Another and is Then Killed by Mistake
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5537 - In another narration, Abu Abdullah (as), was asked about a man who had bequeathed one-third of his wealth and was then killed by mistake.
Imam (as) said: "One-third of his blood money (diyyah) is included in his bequest."
records.arabicText: 5537 - وَ فِي خَبَرٍ آخَرَ : سُئِلَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ أَوْصَى بِثُلُثِ مَالِهِ ثُمَّ قُتِلَ خَطَأً قَالَ "ثُلُثُ دِيَتِهِ دَاخِلٌ فِي وَصِيَّتِهِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.5537 - In another narration, Abu Abdullah (as), was asked about a man who had bequeathed one-third of his wealth and was then killed by mistake.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "One-third of his blood money (diyyah) is included in his bequest."

records.chapter: Chapter on a Man Who Appoints Another as the Guardian of His Children and Their Wealth, Permitting Him at the Time of the Bequest to Invest the Wealth, with the Profits Shared Between Him and Them
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5538 - Muhammad ibn Ya'qub Al-Kulayni, may Allah (swt) be pleased with him, narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad Al-'Asimi from Ali ibn Al-Hasan Al-Mithami from Al-Hasan ibn Ali ibn Yusuf from Mathanna ibn Al-Walid from Muhammad ibn Muslim from Abu Abdullah (as), who was asked about a man who appointed another man as the guardian over his children and their wealth. He also permitted him, in his will, to invest the wealth with the profit being shared between him and the children.
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in that because their father had granted him permission to do so while he was alive."
records.arabicText: 5538 - رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ يَعْقُوبَ اَلْكُلَيْنِيُّ رَضِيَ اَللَّهُ عَنْهُ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ اَلْعَاصِمِيُّ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ اَلْحَسَنِ اَلْمِيثَمِيِّ عَنِ اَلْحَسَنِ بْنِ عَلِيِّ بْنِ يُوسُفَ عَنْ مُثَنَّى بْنِ اَلْوَلِيدِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : أَنَّهُ سُئِلَ عَنْ رَجُلٍ أَوْصَى إِلَى رَجُلٍ بِوُلْدِهِ وَ مَالٍ لَهُمْ وَ أَذِنَ لَهُ عِنْدَ اَلْوَصِيَّةِ أَنْ يَعْمَلَ بِالْمَالِ وَ يَكُونُ اَلرِّبْحُ بَيْنَهُ وَ بَيْنَهُمْ فَقَالَ "لاَ بَأْسَ بِهِ مِنْ أَجْلِ أَنَّ أَبَاهُ قَدْ أَذِنَ لَهُ فِي ذَلِكَ وَ هُوَ حَيٌّ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5538 - Muhammad ibn Ya'qub Al-Kulayni, may Allah (swt) be pleased with him, narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad Al-'Asimi from Ali ibn Al-Hasan Al-Mithami from Al-Hasan ibn Ali ibn Yusuf from Mathanna ibn Al-Walid from Muhammad ibn Muslim from Abu Abdullah (as), who was asked about a man who appointed another man as the guardian over his children and their wealth. He also permitted him, in his will, to invest the wealth with the profit being shared between him and the children.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in that because their father had granted him permission to do so while he was alive."

records.chapter: Chapter on a Man Who Appoints Another as the Guardian of His Children and Their Wealth, Permitting Him at the Time of the Bequest to Invest the Wealth, with the Profits Shared Between Him and Them
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5539 - Ibn Abi Umayr narrated from Abdur Rahman ibn Al-Hajjaj from Khalid Al-Taweel, who said: My father called me when death approached him and said:
"O' my son, take charge of your younger brothers' wealth, invest it, take half of the profit for yourself, and give them the other half. You are not liable for any loss."
After my father's death, my father's concubine brought me before Ibn Abi Layla and said:
"This man is consuming the wealth of my children."
I explained to him what my father had instructed me to do.
Ibn Abi Layla said: "If your father commanded you with something invalid, I will not permit it." Then he had me testify that if I used the wealth, I would be liable for it.
Later, I went to Abu Abdullah (as), and narrated my story to him.
I then asked: "What do you say about this matter?"
Imam (as) said: "As for the ruling of Ibn Abi Layla, I cannot overturn it. But as for what is between you and Allah (swt), the Almighty and Glorious, you are not liable."
records.arabicText: 5539 - وَ رَوَى اِبْنُ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ عَبْدِ اَلرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ اَلْحَجَّاجِ عَنْ خَالِدٍ اَلطَّوِيلِ قَالَ : دَعَانِي أَبِي حِينَ حَضَرَتْهُ اَلْوَفَاةُ فَقَالَ يَا بُنَيَّ اِقْبِضْ مَالَ إِخْوَتِكَ اَلصِّغَارِ وَ اِعْمَلْ بِهِ وَ خُذْ نِصْفَ اَلرِّبْحِ وَ أَعْطِهِمُ اَلنِّصْفَ وَ لَيْسَ عَلَيْكَ ضَمَانٌ فَقَدَّمَتْنِي أُمُّ وَلَدِ أَبِي بَعْدَ وَفَاةِ أَبِي إِلَى اِبْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى فَقَالَتْ إِنَّ هَذَا يَأْكُلُ أَمْوَالَ وُلْدِي قَالَ فَقَصَصْتُ عَلَيْهِ مَا أَمَرَنِي بِهِ أَبِي فَقَالَ اِبْنُ أَبِي لَيْلَى إِنْ كَانَ أَبُوكَ أَمَرَكَ بِالْبَاطِلِ لَمْ أُجِزْهُ ثُمَّ أَشْهَدَ عَلَيَّ اِبْنُ أَبِي لَيْلَى إِنْ أَنَا حَرَّكْتُهُ فَأَنَا لَهُ ضَامِنٌ فَدَخَلْتُ عَلَى أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ بَعْدُ فَاقْتَصَصْتُ عَلَيْهِ قِصَّتِي ثُمَّ قُلْتُ لَهُ مَا تَرَى فَقَالَ "أَمَّا قَوْلُ اِبْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى فَلاَ أَسْتَطِيعُ رَدَّهُ وَ أَمَّا فِيمَا بَيْنَكَ وَ بَيْنَ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ فَلَيْسَ عَلَيْكَ ضَمَانٌ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5539 - Ibn Abi Umayr narrated from Abdur Rahman ibn Al-Hajjaj from Khalid Al-Taweel, who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
My father called me when death approached him and said:
"O' my son, take charge of your younger brothers' wealth, invest it, take half of the profit for yourself, and give them the other half. You are not liable for any loss."
After my father's death, my father's concubine brought me before Ibn Abi Layla and said:
"This man is consuming the wealth of my children."
I explained to him what my father had instructed me to do.
Ibn Abi Layla said: "If your father commanded you with something invalid, I will not permit it." Then he had me testify that if I used the wealth, I would be liable for it.
Later, I went to Abu Abdullah (as), and narrated my story to him.
I then asked: "What do you say about this matter?"
Imam (as) said: "As for the ruling of Ibn Abi Layla, I cannot overturn it. But as for what is between you and Allah (swt), the Almighty and Glorious, you are not liable."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Acknowledgment of Debt by a Sick Person in Favor of an Heir
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5540 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Hisham ibn Salim from Isma'il ibn Jabir, who said: I asked Abu Abdullah (as), about a man who, while ill and in debt, acknowledged a right for one of his heirs.
Imam (as) said: "It is permissible if what he acknowledged is less than one-third (of his wealth)."
records.arabicText: 5540 - رَوَى اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ سَالِمٍ عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ جَابِرٍ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ أَقَرَّ لِوَارِثٍ لَهُ وَ هُوَ مَرِيضٌ بِدَيْنٍ عَلَيْهِ فَقَالَ "يَجُوزُ إِذَا كَانَ اَلَّذِي أَقَرَّ بِهِ دُونَ اَلثُّلُثِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5540 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Hisham ibn Salim from Isma'il ibn Jabir, who said: I asked Abu Abdullah (as), about a man who, while ill and in debt, acknowledged a right for one of his heirs.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "It is permissible if what he acknowledged is less than one-third (of his wealth)."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Acknowledgment of Debt by a Sick Person in Favor of an Heir
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5541 - Hammad narrated from Al-Halabi from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
'I asked Imam (as) about a man who acknowledges a debt to one of his heirs.'
Imam (as) said: "It is permissible if he is solvent (financially capable)."
records.arabicText: 5541 - وَ رَوَى حَمَّادٌ عَنِ اَلْحَلَبِيِّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لَهُ اَلرَّجُلُ يُقِرُّ لِوَارِثٍ بِدَيْنٍ عَلَيْهِ فَقَالَ "يَجُوزُ إِذَا كَانَ مَلِيّاً".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5541 - Hammad narrated from Al-Halabi from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
'I asked Imam (as) about a man who acknowledges a debt to one of his heirs.'
Imam (as) said: "It is permissible if he is solvent (financially capable)."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Acknowledgment of Debt by a Sick Person in Favor of an Heir
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5542 - Safwan ibn Yahya narrated from Mansur ibn Hazim, who said:
'I asked Abu Abdullah (as), about a man who, in his will, acknowledged a debt to one of his heirs.'
Imam (as) said: "If the deceased was in a sound state of health, then give him what was bequeathed to him."
records.arabicText: 5542 - وَ رَوَى صَفْوَانُ بْنُ يَحْيَى عَنْ مَنْصُورِ بْنِ حَازِمٍ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ أَوْصَى لِبَعْضِ وَرَثَتِهِ بِأَنَّ لَهُ عَلَيْهِ دَيْناً فَقَالَ "إِنْ كَانَ اَلْمَيِّتُ مَرْضِيّاً فَأَعْطِهِ اَلَّذِي أَوْصَى لَهُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5542 - Safwan ibn Yahya narrated from Mansur ibn Hazim, who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
'I asked Abu Abdullah (as), about a man who, in his will, acknowledged a debt to one of his heirs.'
Imam (as) said: "If the deceased was in a sound state of health, then give him what was bequeathed to him."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Acknowledgment of Debt by a Sick Person in Favor of an Heir
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5543 - Ali ibn Al-Nu'man narrated from Ibn Muskan from Al-'Alaa, the seller of sabiri (a type of cloth), who said: I asked Abu Abdullah (as), about a woman who entrusted a man with some wealth. When death approached her, she said to him, "The wealth I gave you belongs to so-and-so," and then the woman passed away.
Her heirs came to the man and said: "Our relative had wealth, and we believe it is with you. Swear to us that you have nothing of hers." Should he swear to them?
Imam (as) said: "If she was trustworthy in his view, then he should swear. But if she was suspect, then he should not swear and should leave the matter as it is. Indeed, she only has a right to one-third of her wealth."
records.arabicText: 5543 - وَ رَوَى عَلِيُّ بْنُ اَلنُّعْمَانِ عَنِ اِبْنِ مُسْكَانَ عَنِ اَلْعَلاَءِ بَيَّاعِ اَلسَّابِرِيِّ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنِ اِمْرَأَةٍ اِسْتَوْدَعَتْ رَجُلاً مَالاً فَلَمَّا حَضَرَهَا اَلْمَوْتُ قَالَتْ لَهُ إِنَّ اَلْمَالَ اَلَّذِي دَفَعْتُهُ إِلَيْكَ لِفُلاَنَةَ وَ مَاتَتِ اَلْمَرْأَةُ فَأَتَى أَوْلِيَاؤُهَا اَلرَّجُلَ وَ قَالُوا إِنَّهُ كَانَ لِصَاحِبَتِنَا مَالٌ لاَ نَرَاهُ إِلاَّ عِنْدَكَ فَاحْلِفْ لَنَا مَا قِبَلَكَ شَيْءٌ أَ فَيَحْلِفُ لَهُمْ فَقَالَ "إِنْ كَانَتْ مَأْمُونَةً عِنْدَهُ فَلْيَحْلِفْ وَ إِنْ كَانَتْ مُتَّهَمَةً فَلاَ يَحْلِفُ وَ يَضَعُ اَلْأَمْرَ عَلَى مَا كَانَ فَإِنَّمَا لَهَا مِنْ مَالِهَا ثُلُثُهُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5543 - Ali ibn Al-Nu'man narrated from Ibn Muskan from Al-'Alaa, the seller of sabiri (a type of cloth), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I asked Abu Abdullah (as), about a woman who entrusted a man with some wealth. When death approached her, she said to him, "The wealth I gave you belongs to so-and-so," and then the woman passed away.
Her heirs came to the man and said: "Our relative had wealth, and we believe it is with you. Swear to us that you have nothing of hers." Should he swear to them?
Imam (as) said: "If she was trustworthy in his view, then he should swear. But if she was suspect, then he should not swear and should leave the matter as it is. Indeed, she only has a right to one-third of her wealth."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Acknowledgment of Emancipation or Debt by Some of the Heirs
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5544 - Yunus ibn Abd Al-Rahman narrated from Mansur ibn Hazim from Abu Abdullah (as), regarding a man who died and left behind a slave. One of his children testified that their father had freed the slave.
Imam (as) said: "His testimony is accepted, and he is not held financially responsible. However, the slave must work to compensate the share that belonged to the other heirs."
records.arabicText: 5544 - رَوَى يُونُسُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اَلرَّحْمَنِ عَنْ مَنْصُورِ بْنِ حَازِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي رَجُلٍ مَاتَ وَ تَرَكَ عَبْداً فَشَهِدَ بَعْضُ وُلْدِهِ أَنَّ أَبَاهُ أَعْتَقَهُ فَقَالَ "تَجُوزُ عَلَيْهِ شَهَادَتُهُ وَ لاَ يُغْرَمُ وَ يُسْتَسْعَى اَلْغُلاَمُ فِيمَا كَانَ لِغَيْرِهِ مِنَ اَلْوَرَثَةِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5544 - Yunus ibn Abd Al-Rahman narrated from Mansur ibn Hazim from Abu Abdullah (as), regarding a man who died and left behind a slave. One of his children testified that their father had freed the slave.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "His testimony is accepted, and he is not held financially responsible. However, the slave must work to compensate the share that belonged to the other heirs."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Acknowledgment of Emancipation or Debt by Some of the Heirs
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5545 - Ibn Abi Umayr narrated from Muhammad ibn Abi Hamzah and Husayn ibn Uthman from Ishaq ibn Ammar from Abu Abdullah (as), regarding a man who died, and some of his heirs acknowledged a debt owed by the deceased to another man.
Imam (as) said: "That acknowledgment is binding upon the share of the one who made the acknowledgment."
records.arabicText: 5545 - وَ رَوَى اِبْنُ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي حَمْزَةَ وَ حُسَيْنِ بْنِ عُثْمَانَ عَنْ إِسْحَاقَ بْنِ عَمَّارٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي رَجُلٍ مَاتَ فَأَقَرَّ بَعْضُ وَرَثَتِهِ لِرَجُلٍ بِدَيْنٍ فَقَالَ "يَلْزَمُهُ ذَلِكَ فِي حِصَّتِهِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5545 - Ibn Abi Umayr narrated from Muhammad ibn Abi Hamzah and Husayn ibn Uthman from Ishaq ibn Ammar from Abu Abdullah (as), regarding a man who died, and some of his heirs acknowledged a debt owed by the deceased to another man.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "That acknowledgment is binding upon the share of the one who made the acknowledgment."
